events.summary: sTec board determines to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of the company.
WDC CEO John Coyne expresses interest in exploring SSD expansion during an industry lunch with sTec founder Manouch Moshayedi.
events.boundary_note: General and exploratory; no specific transaction terms proposed.

events.summary: WDC SVP Michael Hajeck holds lunch meeting with Manouch Moshayedi, inquiring about founders' plans to continue running sTec.
events.boundary_note: No further discussion until later communications.

events.summary: Company A's investment bank contacts sTec management to request a preliminary meeting and provides a draft NDA.
notes: Meeting was scheduled but subsequently cancelled.

events.summary: Balch Hill Partners files Schedule 13D with the SEC disclosing acquisition of 6.4% of sTec outstanding shares.
Balch Hill files amended Schedule 13D and issues letter to sTec board urging cost reduction, SAS focus, and exploration of strategic alternatives.
Company B's President meets with Manouch Moshayedi and states intention to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B management.
Company C representatives express potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets of sTec's business.
Company D's head of corporate development contacts sTec management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.
sTec board discusses a strategic review process and directs a confidential outreach to strategic and financial buyers.
events.boundary_note: Board-level process initiation preceding BofA outreach on April 1, 2013.

events.summary: sTec enters into engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
notes: Board approved retention on March 26, 2013; engagement letter executed March 28.

events.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch begins contacting potential acquirers of sTec; discussions with 18 prospective acquirers total.
sTec enters into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
notes: NDA included a dont ask, dont waive provision.

events.summary: sTec enters into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
sTec enters into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
notes: NDA included a dont ask, dont waive provision.

events.summary: sTec enters into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
sTec enters into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
events.boundary_note: Addendum to existing NDA, not a new NDA.

events.summary: Company F declines management presentation invitation, indicating only interested in limited, select assets.
events.drop_reason_text: Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec

events.summary: Company E indicates only interested in limited, select assets; special committee decides not to continue discussions.
events.drop_reason_text: Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec

events.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sends process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
BofA Merrill Lynch sends process letter to Company G requesting non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.
Company D provides verbal indication of interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
Company H contacts BofA Merrill Lynch expressing interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
sTec enters into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
notes: NDA included a dont ask, dont waive provision.

events.summary: WDC submits written indication of interest to acquire the company at $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash.
Company G indicates it would not continue in the process.
events.drop_reason_text: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process

events.summary: Company D submits written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
Company H submits written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at $5.00-$5.75 per share in cash.
BofA Merrill Lynch sends final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
notes: Sent after the May 16 board meeting, at the direction of the board.

events.summary: Company H states it remains interested but is not able to increase its indicated value range.
events.drop_reason_text: Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range
events.boundary_note: Company H was effectively out of the process at this point, having been told its range was insufficient.

events.summary: WDC submits written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash, with mark-up of merger agreement and ancillary agreements.
notes: Contemplated signing definitive documentation by June 3, 2013.

events.summary: Board directs BofA Merrill Lynch to request best and final proposals from WDC and Company D by May 30, 2013.
WDC verbally reconfirms $9.15 per share as its best possible price, reiterating concerns regarding price.
WDC communicates it is reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec and is not prepared to move forward; discontinues due diligence.
events.drop_reason_text: WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time
events.boundary_note: Temporary withdrawal; WDC re-engaged by June 10.

events.summary: Company D indicates it would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence and disengages from the process.
events.drop_reason_text: Company D would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process

events.summary: WDC re-engages after internal reconsideration and submits revised written indication of interest at $6.60-$7.10 per share.
events.boundary_note: WDC had previously withdrawn on May 31. Price reduced from $9.15 to $6.60-$7.10.

events.summary: WDC submits revised written indication of interest at $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash, citing changed business factors and bidding strategy.
WDC submits written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash, described as WDC's best and final price.
notes: Indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

events.summary: sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub execute the merger agreement at $6.85 per share in cash.
WDC and sTec announce in a joint press release the execution of the merger agreement, prior to opening of trading.

exclusions.explanation: Board and special committee meetings discussing process status, fiduciary duties, and deliberations. These are discussion events, not discrete actionable M&A events.
BofA Merrill Lynch fairness opinion methodology, comparable companies/transactions analysis, and DCF analysis. These support the opinion delivery but are not independent M&A events.
sTec business performance, workforce reductions, and operating context. Not M&A process events.
BofA Merrill Lynch fee arrangements and prior relationship disclosures. Financial details, not process events.
Board rationale for recommending the merger and merger agreement terms discussion. These are deal terms and board reasoning, not discrete events.
Company C, Company E, Company F expressed interest only in limited, select assets. Their asset-only interest is captured in drop events. No separate partial-company proposal events warranted.

coverage_notes: target_sale: extracted (evt_001 board initiates strategic review, evt_013 BofA begins contacting acquirers)
target_sale_public: NOT FOUND -- sTec did not separately announce its strategic review publicly; the public announcement was the June 24 merger agreement announcement
bidder_sale: NOT FOUND -- no bidder-side sale process described
bidder_interest: extracted (evt_002 WDC 2011, evt_003 WDC summer 2012, evt_004 Company A, evt_008 Company B, evt_010 Company C, evt_011 Company D, evt_025 Company H)
activist_sale: extracted (evt_006 Balch Hill 13D filing, evt_007 Balch Hill amended 13D and letter)
sale_press_release: NOT FOUND -- no separate sTec announcement of sale process; the target_sale was confidential
bid_press_release: extracted (evt_042 joint WDC/sTec press release June 24, 2013)
ib_retention: extracted (evt_012 BofA Merrill Lynch engagement March 28, 2013)
nda: extracted (evt_014 Company E, evt_015 Company D, evt_016 Company F, evt_017 Company G, evt_018 WDC addendum, evt_026 Company H)

proposal: extracted (evt_024 Company D verbal >$5.60, evt_027 WDC $6.60-$7.10, evt_029 Company D $5.75, evt_030 Company H $5.00-$5.75, evt_033 WDC $9.15, evt_035 WDC verbal $9.15 reconfirm, evt_039 WDC $6.60-$7.10 revised, evt_040 WDC $6.85 best and final)

drop: extracted (evt_005 Company A, evt_009 Company B, evt_019 Company C, evt_020 Company F, evt_021 Company E, evt_028 Company G, evt_032 Company H, evt_036 WDC temporary withdrawal, evt_037 Company D disengage)
final_round_inf_ann: extracted (evt_022 April 23 process letters to WDC/Company D, evt_023 April 26 process letter to Company G)
final_round_inf: NOT FOUND -- no explicit informal final round submission event distinct from proposals already captured
final_round_ann: extracted (evt_031 final round process letters with draft merger agreement to WDC/Company D)
final_round: NOT FOUND -- the final round submissions are captured as proposals (evt_033 WDC $9.15)
final_round_ext_ann: extracted (evt_034 board directs best and final from WDC and Company D by May 30)
final_round_ext: NOT FOUND -- the best and final responses are captured as proposals (evt_035 WDC reconfirm $9.15)
executed: extracted (evt_041 merger agreement execution June 23, 2013)
terminated: NOT FOUND -- merger was not terminated in this filing narrative
restarted: extracted (evt_038 WDC re-engages after May 31 withdrawal)
